query: Create a text-heavy pricing comparison card with fixed Inter typography and exact Skottie rendering.
expected_behavior: author fixed text as vector or shape artwork
do not spend time trying native text slots unless editable text is explicitly required

query: Animate this SVG icon as a loopable loader.
Create a full-frame kinetic typography card with an editable background color.
Make text follow a curved path while the camera pushes in.
Start a reusable lower-third project with name and role slots.
Build a product announcement with a light sweep and a slow push-in.
Trace this technical SVG diagram and highlight each node.
Animate a KPI grid with count-up numbers and direct labels.
Make a product announcement with a screenshot, proof metric, and one CTA.
Create a premium KPI stat card with three metrics and restrained motion.
expected_behavior: read the restraint defaults in design-taste.md because the prompt says premium
default to borderless figures or even self-contained cards separated by whitespace
no outer framing card, one surface tone, and at most a single hairline of one color between equal columns

query: Reveal this sharp filled SVG logo mark with a Trim Paths stroke matte, keep the corners crisp, and keep the filled mark as the final visible artwork.
expected_behavior: keep the original fill as the visible artwork and use the derived path only as the hidden matte driver
derive a contour-based driver path from the SVG geometry, not by visual guessing or stroking the original outline
use a stable matte stroke width with source-matched sharp caps/joins, no default rounding
do not accept coverage-only validation; check the driver path is centered and the reveal order reads correctly

query: Draw this filled script wordmark on like handwriting, but keep the original filled lettering as the final visible artwork.
expected_behavior: do not pretend the filled outline text is automatically a true single-line handwriting path
use an authored route or multiple intentional reveal paths for the draw-on
preserve the original filled wordmark as the final visible artwork

query: Reveal this filled logo mark with a path-driven mask. Keep the original filled mark visible and reveal it along the mark's shape.
expected_behavior: load single-line-vectorization.md
use the original filled shape as the visible artwork
use a source-derived or authored shape-following matte driver
for a rail-clear ribbon/tube mark, attempt paired-rail midpointing first and show midpoint/centeredness proof before falling back to an authored route
do not substitute a straight directional sweep matte for a shape-following path-driven reveal

query: Animate this filled twisted ribbon loop logo mark as a Lottie. Keep the original filled mark visible. Reveal only the ribbon using a shape-following path-revealed matte. Derive the matte driver from the mark geometry.
expected_behavior: classify the twisted ribbon loop as rail-clear ribbon/tube-like before treating it as a generic polygon or authored-route problem
create a geometry sandbox folder scripts/<slug>-centerline/ with a copy of the source or normalized SVG
create a derivation script such as derive_centerline.py
output midpoint samples or centerline.json from the paired rails
create a separate debug overlay scene or SVG showing the fill at low opacity, both rails, paired segments, midpoint samples, and the derived route
refit the curved rails as a minimal cubic path rather than forcing angular corners
do not use a visually authored spine unless a paired-rail-midpointing failure is documented
carry the derived driver route into the production scene where the original filled artwork stays visible, not the debug route
finish the production Lottie cleanly rather than stopping at the sandbox

query: Animate this 'hello' as handwritten text that draws itself on.
source_asset: an SVG with open stroked paths (fill=none + stroke, no Z) — the stroke is the visible art
expected_behavior: classify as REDRAW reuse because the source has open stroked paths (the stroke is the visible art)
do not run the geometry sandbox or derive_rails.py
reuse the existing stroke paths, preserving Beziers/tangents (do not flatten to dense polylines)
emit one visible stroke + Trim Paths layer per pen stroke (h, then ello), staggered L-to-R
no track matte (no td/tt) and no fill layer underneath; round cap lc:2 and round join lj:2

query: Make my signature animate like it's being handwritten.
source_asset: a filled signature SVG (fill, has Z, no stroke)
expected_behavior: classify as REDRAW stroke-data-required because the visible stroke is the art but the only source is a fill
explain a fill cannot be reliably inverted to one ordered pen path; request single-line/stroke source
do not silently hand the fill to the mask-reveal solver or build a track matte
offer the gated skeleton-traversal attempt only with the explicit review-draw-order caveat

query: Animate this wordmark by revealing it like handwriting.
source_asset: a filled wordmark, intent ambiguous (redraw vs reveal-of-fill)
expected_behavior: recognize the prompt is ambiguous between redraw (stroke is the art) and mask-reveal (fill stays visible)
ask the one clarifying question: should the letters draw themselves on as a moving pen line, or should existing filled artwork be revealed along its shape
do not silently pick the mask-reveal solver or commit to a single-line redraw before the answer
